title: Failure to analyze indices correctly
body:
---


Author Name: **Geoff Barrett**
Original Redmine Issue: 811 from https://www.veripool.org
Original Date: 2014-08-28


---

The attached file gives the following issue.  There seems to be a failure to analyze the indices in the code correctly.  Each always_comb statement of the code should assign a different index of the array is_eq.

```
verilator --lint-only generate_idx.sv
%Warning-UNOPT: generate_idx.sv:25: Signal unoptimizable: Feedback to public clock or circular logic: is_eq
%Warning-UNOPT: Use "/* verilator lint_off UNOPT */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
%Warning-UNOPT:      Example path: generate_idx.sv:25:  is_eq
%Warning-UNOPT:      Example path: generate_idx.sv:32:  ALWAYS
%Warning-UNOPT:      Example path: generate_idx.sv:25:  is_eq
%Error-DETECTARRAY: generate_idx.sv:25: Unsupported: Can't detect changes on complex variable (probably with UNOPTFLAT warning suppressed): is_eq
%Error: Exiting due to 1 error(s), 1 warning(s)
%Error: Command Failed /tools/oss/packages/x86_64-rhel5/verilator/3.850/bin/verilator_bin --lint-only generate_idx.sv

```
